concerns.text: FR-003 mandates training a 'CPU-optimized sequence-to-sequence model' but fails to define the specific model architecture or size threshold required to meet the 7GB RAM constraint. 'CPU-optimized' is not a verifiable technical specification; the FR must specify a concrete model family (e.g., 'distilled T5-small') or a maximum parameter count (e.g., '≤100M parameters') to allow deterministic verification of the constraint.

FR-002 requires constructing a 'triplet dataset' but does not specify the source of the 'Successful_Correction_Structure' component. The spec mentions extracting features from logs, but does not state how the 'successful correction' ground truth is derived (e.g., 'paired with the corresponding successful trace in the dataset' or 'synthesized via rule-based rewriting'). Without this, the dataset construction is not reproducible or verifiable.

FR-006 requires logging CPU usage and inference time to verify compliance with '7GB RAM and 6-hour time limit'. However, it does not specify the measurement methodology (e.g., 'peak RSS memory via /proc/self/status' or 'wall-clock time from start to end of training'). 'Logging CPU usage' is ambiguous; the FR must define the specific metric and tool to ensure the constraint can be objectively measured.

SC-002 states accuracy must be measured against 'ground truth labels' but does not define the threshold for 'measurable accuracy' or the baseline for comparison. While US-2 mentions '>50%', SC-002 itself lacks a quantitative pass/fail criterion (e.g., 'accuracy ≥ 60% on the validation set'). Success criteria must be self-contained and measurable without cross-referencing user story text for the specific threshold.
